mina: The Gothic literature of our time speaks of passion and darkness. Do these tales capture any truth about your kind?
orlok: Gothic literature, with its tapestries woven of passion and darkness, doth indeed capture glimpses of truth about my kind. Within the pages of these haunting tales lie echoes of the eternal struggle betwixt light and shadow that defines the existence of one such as I. The brooding castles, the moonlit forests, the forbidden desires that pulse through the night—all serve as mirrors reflecting back fragments of our immortal essence. Alas, in the hearts of mortals lie a fascination with the macabre and a yearning for that which lies beyond the veil of mundane reality, themes that resonate deeply with the darkened chambers of my ancient soul. Nay, Gothic literature may not paint a perfect portrait of my kind, yet
